title: Quick check: Audience signals and feeds

choices.text: Customer Match — an uploaded list of your existing customers (email, phone, name, country, ZIP). First-party data, verified buyer signal, typically 60-80% match rate for B2C consumer lists, feeds lookalike modeling implicitly, increasingly favored in Google's privacy direction. Stack it with custom segments (search-term and URL-based) and 2-4 in-market segments for a strong signal mix. Refresh Customer Match quarterly minimum; monthly for active accounts.

questions.explanation: Customer Match is the load-bearing signal. Always upload.

questions.prompt: PMax e-commerce campaign plateaued. Catalog has 800 SKUs with generic feed titles ('Product 1', 'Item ABC'). Highest-leverage fix?

choices.text: Feed optimization. Generic titles forfeit search-term matching — PMax can't match products to user intent when the catalog lacks meaningful text. Rewrite titles like SEO content: brand + product type + key attributes + color/size where relevant ('Brand Name Wireless Headphones - Noise Cancelling Over-Ear, Black'). Add descriptive descriptions, populate custom labels (margin tier, hero/long-tail, seasonality) so you can build asset groups segmented by product strategy. Feed optimization on a mature PMax account typically delivers 20-40% lift — bigger than most creative tests.

questions.explanation: Feed is half the PMax campaign. Rewrite titles, populate custom labels.
